SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.equipment.powergeneration.gasturbine

Class GasTurbinePerformanceMap

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.equipment.powergeneration.gasturbine.GasTurbinePerformanceMap
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class GasTurbinePerformanceMap extends Object implements Serializable
Part-load and ambient-correction performance map for a gas turbine.

The map combines three effects to map ISO rated performance from GasTurbineSpec to actual site conditions and operating load:

  • Part-load heat rate — polynomial in load fraction (load = actual power / rated power at site conditions). Default coefficients reproduce the classical aeroderivative shape: ~+5 % heat rate at 75 % load, ~+18 % at 50 %, ~+45 % at 30 %.
  • Ambient temperature correction — linear loss of available power above 15 °C ISO (~0.7 %/°C for aeroderivatives, ~0.5 %/°C for industrial frames) and a small heat-rate penalty.
  • Site pressure / altitude correction — power scales linearly with ambient pressure ratio relative to 1.013 bara.

All coefficients can be overridden if vendor maps are available.

Version:
$Id: $Id
Author:
neqsim

    Description
    GasTurbinePerformanceMap()

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static GasTurbinePerformanceMap
    forIndustrial()
    Create a map for an industrial / heavy-frame turbine with flatter part-load curve and lower ambient sensitivity.

    fromSpec(GasTurbineSpec spec)
    Create a map matching the supplied catalog spec (selects industrial vs aeroderivative defaults).
    double
    getAvailablePower(double ratedPowerW, double ambientTemperatureK, double ambientPressureBara)
    Maximum continuous power available at the given ambient conditions.

    Thermal efficiency at a given load fraction and ambient temperature.
    double
    getExhaustFlow(double ratedExhaustFlowKgPerS, double loadFraction)
    Exhaust mass flow at the given load fraction (approximately linear with load fraction for aeroderivatives, slightly less than linear for industrial frames).
    double

    Exhaust temperature at the given load fraction.

    Override the part-load polynomial coefficients.

  • Constructor Details

    • GasTurbinePerformanceMap

      public GasTurbinePerformanceMap()
      Create a default map suitable for a low-emission aeroderivative turbine.

  • Method Details

    • forIndustrial

      public static GasTurbinePerformanceMap forIndustrial()
      Create a map for an industrial / heavy-frame turbine with flatter part-load curve and lower ambient sensitivity.
      Returns:
      new map instance configured for industrial turbines

    • fromSpec

      public static GasTurbinePerformanceMap fromSpec(GasTurbineSpec spec)
      Create a map matching the supplied catalog spec (selects industrial vs aeroderivative defaults).
      Parameters:
      spec - gas turbine catalog entry
      Returns:
      new map configured for the spec type

    • getAvailablePower

      public double getAvailablePower(double ratedPowerW, double ambientTemperatureK, double ambientPressureBara)
      Maximum continuous power available at the given ambient conditions.
      Parameters:
      ratedPowerW - ISO rated power [W]
      ambientTemperatureK - ambient temperature [K]
      ambientPressureBara - ambient pressure [bara]
      Returns:
      site-corrected available power [W]

    • getHeatRate

      public double getHeatRate(double isoHeatRateKJPerKWh, double loadFraction, double ambientTemperatureK)
      Effective heat rate at a given load fraction and ambient temperature.
      Parameters:
      isoHeatRateKJPerKWh - ISO heat rate [kJ/kWh]
      loadFraction - fraction of site-corrected available power (0–1.1)
      ambientTemperatureK - ambient temperature [K]
      Returns:
      effective heat rate [kJ/kWh]
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if loadFraction is non-positive

    • getEfficiency

      public double getEfficiency(double isoHeatRateKJPerKWh, double loadFraction, double ambientTemperatureK)
      Thermal efficiency at a given load fraction and ambient temperature.
      Parameters:
      isoHeatRateKJPerKWh - ISO heat rate [kJ/kWh]
      loadFraction - load fraction
      ambientTemperatureK - ambient temperature [K]
      Returns:
      thermal efficiency (0–1)

    • getExhaustFlow

      public double getExhaustFlow(double ratedExhaustFlowKgPerS, double loadFraction)
      Exhaust mass flow at the given load fraction (approximately linear with load fraction for aeroderivatives, slightly less than linear for industrial frames).
      Parameters:
      ratedExhaustFlowKgPerS - ISO exhaust flow [kg/s]
      loadFraction - load fraction
      Returns:
      exhaust flow [kg/s]

    • getExhaustTemperature

      public double getExhaustTemperature(double ratedExhaustTemperatureK, double loadFraction)
      Exhaust temperature at the given load fraction. Exhaust T typically increases as load drops on a two-shaft engine because TET is held constant while air flow drops.
      Parameters:
      ratedExhaustTemperatureK - ISO exhaust temperature [K]
      loadFraction - load fraction
      Returns:
      exhaust temperature [K]

    • getMinLoadFraction

      public double getMinLoadFraction()
      Get minimum stable load fraction.
      Returns:
      minimum load fraction (0–1)

    • setMinLoadFraction

      public void setMinLoadFraction(double minLoadFraction)
      Set minimum stable load fraction (engineering judgement / OEM limit).
      Parameters:
      minLoadFraction - minimum load fraction (0–1)

    • setHeatRatePolynomial

      public void setHeatRatePolynomial(double a0, double a1, double a2)
      Override the part-load polynomial coefficients.
      Parameters:
      a0 - constant term
      a1 - linear term
      a2 - quadratic term

    • setAmbientCorrection

      public void setAmbientCorrection(double powerLossPerKelvin, double heatRateRisePerKelvin)
      Override the ambient temperature corrections.
      Parameters:
      powerLossPerKelvin - fraction of rated power lost per K above ISO
      heatRateRisePerKelvin - fractional heat-rate rise per K above ISO
